title: Podman running root with uid/gid mapping on --rootfs
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Trying to start a container with the --rootfs flag, and with the uid/gid mapping. 
I am trying to get a container to start with network bridges, however, I want root in the container to be a mapped user from the host. 

This does not seem to be working. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. 
`sudo podman --root /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root/ --runroot /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root/ run --name test -it --rm --uidmap 0:1000:1 --uidmap 1:100000:65536 --gidmap 0:1001:1 --gidmap 1:100000:65536 --read-only --rootfs /path/to/rootfs /bin/bash`


**Describe the results you received:**
It does not start and errors with the following: 

```
Error: error stat'ing file `/data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root/overlay-containers/eaad35ba246dd372a8fe588dd1181803eaacbdcd066e63c7b670d8fed3b9c368/userdata/hosts`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
```



It also shows other files if I keep running it:
```
Error: error stat'ing file `/data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root/overlay-containers/8cba57fd569271533e323abd1073f54c2faf5cc32a8bc0d34756c4d7cc32e621/userdata/resolv.conf`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container to start successfully as my user mapped into it. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

If I simple remove the mappings, the container starts:

```
 sudo podman --root /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root/ --runroot /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root/ run --name test -it --rm  --rootfs /data/gpu/rootfs /bin/bash
root@fac7879bae8f:/#
```
AND

This seems to work with images (when not using --rootfs) https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10314




**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.0-dev
API Version:  3.2.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.16
Git Commit:   2039be00d12afaab84659619c47a463cacb039f5
Built:        Mon Apr 26 21:54:33 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

DEBU[0000] container 2bd773c6b4e9d461b62a25d67220ade914753e34720b6d7f8eee7534e191030b failed to unmount /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root/overlay-containers/2bd773c6b4e9d461b62a25d67220ade914753e34720b6d7f8eee7534e191030b/userdata/shm : invalid argument
DEBU[0000] container 2bd773c6b4e9d461b62a25d67220ade914753e34720b6d7f8eee7534e191030b failed to unmount /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root/overlay-containers/2bd773c6b4e9d461b62a25d67220ade914753e34720b6d7f8eee7534e191030b/userdata/shm : invalid argument
DEBU[0000] ExitCode msg: "error stat'ing file `/data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root/overlay-containers/2bd773c6b4e9d461b62a25d67220ade914753e34720b6d7f8eee7534e191030b/userdata/resolv.conf`: permission denied: oci permission denied"
Error: error stat'ing file `/data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root/overlay-containers/2bd773c6b4e9d461b62a25d67220ade914753e34720b6d7f8eee7534e191030b/userdata/resolv.conf`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
```

Do your root and runroot directories (and their parent directories) have execute permission for group and all set? With the `--uidmap` and `--gidmap` flags you're forcing Podman to access those directories as a non-root user, and so they need access to said directories.

How do you propose I do that? 

Do I create the directories with root first, then chown it to a group that includes the user of my `--uidmap` and `--gidmap`and root ?
You do not need to change group ownership - just ensure that +x is set for Group and All on any directories you create, and all their parents up to `/`.

Okay so it seems based of your suggestions I did get it to work by doing `create` instead of `run`:

```
$ sudo podman --root /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root/ --runroot /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root/ create --name test -it --rm --uidmap 0:1000:1 --uidmap 1:100000:65536 --gidmap 0:1001:1 --gidmap 1:100000:65536 -v /tmp:/tmp --network test --read-only --rootfs /data/rootfs /bin/bash
a96e2ad94df5f8f9bf9d95bba091257aed7a148f8ff0632d944a0c40dc70bbd1
$ sudo chmod -R o+x data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root && sudo chmod -R o+x data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root
$ sudo chmod -R g+x data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root && sudo chmod -R g+x data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root
$ sudo podman --root /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root/ --runroot /data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root/ start -ia test
bash: /root/.bashrc: Permission denied
root@a96e2ad94df5:/#
root@a96e2ad94df5:/# hostname -I
10.89.0.2
root@a96e2ad94df5:/#
```

I will also try an ACL approach where I can setup default access: 
https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/1314/how-to-set-default-file-permissions-for-all-folders-files-in-a-directory

Are there any other approaches there might be that you know of ?

Once we have a shifting file system, which was recently merged into the upstream kernel, although it does not currently work with overlay we will be in better state.

@giuseppe WDYT?

Also is there any way I can restrict this more to the particular user I want running the container?

Or is my only option to do what I did above:

```
$ sudo chmod -R o+x data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root && sudo chmod -R o+x data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root
$ sudo chmod -R g+x data/gpu/squash-test/podman-run-root && sudo chmod -R g+x data/gpu/squash-test/podman-root
```

when rootfs is used Podman doesn't manage the storage at all.

Podman expects rootfs to already have the correct files ownership.  I don't think this will change with idmapped mounts, unless it is configured before Podman runs.
